subject: FW: The Rockwood Report, May 2008
body:
From: Bob Rockwood [mailto:racersblue@earthlink.net] 
Sent: Thursday, May 01, 2008 9:24 AM
To: Bob Rockwood
Subject: The Rockwood Report, May 2008

 

HUH?

 

In late March 2008, the FAA announced that Gulfstream Aerospace became
the first aircraft manufacturer to be granted FAA Organizational
Designation Authorization for initial phase manufacturing at the
company's production facility in Savannah, Ga. In other words, to some
degree, Gulfstream gets to act on behalf of the FAA in reviewing its own
products, their design and manufacture.

 

I grew up on a little farm, and we called this "letting the fox into the
henhouse".

 

In no way do I mean this to disparage Gulfstream Aerospace, but come on!
Is not Southwest Airlines being faced with a $10.0 million fine because
of a miscue under the voluntary safety compliance program? The
government studies, audits and finger pointing will go on for some time
on this one, and there will never be a clear villain. However, the
bottom line is no clear lines were drawn and the FAA, the governing
body, outsourced some of its responsibility to Southwest, the governed
body. 

 

Back on the farm we called this "letting the inmates run the asylum".

 

It is time we stopped trying to keep government spending in check by
eliminating the services government is good for. Despite the thinking of
the past 30 years, not everything is about compromise. Sometimes a rule
is a rule and needs to be enforced from the outside.

 

Bob Rockwood

202-682-4000 (Main phone number)

540-554-8113 (Direct phone number)

racersblue@earthlink.net (Direct email)

www.bristolassociates.com (Web address)

 

If the message above was not intended for you, please destroy it and
don't use it.
body_html:
From: Bob Rockwood [mailto:racersblue@earthlink.net]
Sent: Thursday, May 01, 2008 9:24 AM
To: Bob Rockwood
Subject: The Rockwood Report, May 2008

 

HUH?

 

In late March 2008, the FAA announced that Gulfstream Aerospace became the first aircraft manufacturer to be granted FAA Organizational Designation Authorization for initial phase manufacturing at the company’s production facility in Savannah, Ga. In other words, to some degree, Gulfstream gets to act on behalf of the FAA in reviewing its own products, their design and manufacture.

 

I grew up on a little farm, and we called this “letting the fox into the henhouse”.

 

In no way do I mean this to disparage Gulfstream Aerospace, but come on! Is not Southwest Airlines being faced with a $10.0 million fine because of a miscue under the voluntary safety compliance program? The government studies, audits and finger pointing will go on for some time on this one, and there will never be a clear villain. However, the bottom line is no clear lines were drawn and the FAA, the governing body, outsourced some of its responsibility to Southwest, the governed body.

 

Back on the farm we called this “letting the inmates run the asylum”.

 

It is time we stopped trying to keep government spending in check by eliminating the services government is good for. Despite the thinking of the past 30 years, not everything is about compromise. Sometimes a rule is a rule and needs to be enforced from the outside.

 

Bob Rockwood
